slides.title: The muscle you keep is *the freedom* you'll have.
slides.sub: Strength is one of the clearest markers of aging well. Here is how to build it, at any age.

slides.title: Muscle fades with age
slides.body: From your 30s on you slowly lose muscle. It is called sarcopenia, and *it speeds up* in later decades.

slides.title: Muscle isn't vanity
slides.body: Muscle means balance, independence and a *place to store glucose*, which can support steadier metabolic health.

slides.title: Two sessions a week
slides.body: Two strength sessions weekly is enough to start, and *it is never too late*. Even 80-year-olds gain muscle in studies.

slides.title: Lift, carry, climb
slides.body: Lift weights, carry the groceries, climb stairs, do sit-to-stands. Then *add a little* over time.

slides.title: Then let it rebuild
slides.body: Muscle grows on the recovery, not the lift. *Protein and rest* are how the work pays off.

slides.title: Build strength that *lasts decades*.
slides.body: Vibe Health scores the movement, food and recovery behind your strength, all in one simple daily number.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
The muscle you build now is the independence you'll have at 80. 💪

Two strength sessions a week is enough to start, and it's never too late: even 80-year-olds gain. Add protein and recovery and let it rebuild. 🔖

Vibe Health turns the work behind your strength into one daily score → link in bio.
youtube_title: Why Building Muscle Now Keeps You Independent at 80
